tools.name: Bash
tools.description: Execute a bash command. Working directory: /workspace/group. Returns stdout + stderr.

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Read a file and return its contents with line numbers.

tools.name: Write
tools.description: Write content to a file (creates parent directories as needed).

tools.name: Edit
tools.description: Replace a specific string in a file. old_string must appear exactly once in the file.

old_string.description: Exact text to replace (must be unique in the file)

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern.

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Search file contents using ripgrep. Returns matching lines with file:line context.

flags.description: Extra rg flags e.g. "-i" (case insensitive), "-l" (filenames only)

tools.name: WebFetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL and return its text content (HTML stripped). Use for static pages without JavaScript interactions.

tools.name: agent-browser
tools.description: Full browser automation for JavaScript-heavy sites, logins, and interactions. Use Bash with agent-browser commands to: open URLs, take screenshots, click elements, fill forms. Requires using Bash(command: 'agent-browser ...').

command.description: Agent-browser command to run via Bash: open <url>, snapshot, snapshot -i, click @e1, fill @e2 'text', get text @e1, screenshot file.png, close

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a WhatsApp text message to the chat right now (before finishing). Use for progress updates.

tools.name: send_voice
tools.description: Send a WhatsApp voice note (spoken audio). REQUIRED when the user asks for audio, a voice note, or to hear something spoken — call this with the content; do not reply with text only. Also use when the user sent a voice message. Keep text short — under ~200 words. The system delivers the voice note first, then your next message; do not claim 'I sent you a voice note' in that message — use short follow-ups only (e.g. 'Summary above.').

text.description: Text to speak aloud as a voice note

voice.description: Optional speaker or language: Ryan, Aiden, Vivian, Serena, Uncle_Fu, Dylan, Eric, Ono_Anna, Sohee, or French for French TTS (default: Ryan)

tools.name: send_image
tools.description: Send an image to the WhatsApp chat so the user sees it. REQUIRED after agent-browser screenshot: call this with the screenshot path (e.g. page.png) before replying with text — otherwise the user never sees the image. Path must be under /workspace/group.

path.description: Path to the image file, e.g. page.png or /workspace/group/page.png

tools.name: ask_boss
tools.description: Ask the user (the boss) for guidance when stuck, unsure, or before doing something risky/irreversible. Your question is sent to the chat. Stop and wait for their reply — do not proceed until you have it. Use when: ambiguous request, destructive action, sensitive data, or you lack context.

question.description: Clear question or context for the user to respond to

tools.name: schedule_task
tools.description: Schedule a recurring or one-time task that runs as an agent.

prompt.description: What the agent should do when the task runs

schedule_type.description: cron=time-based, interval=every N ms, once=run once

schedule_value.description: cron: "0 9 * * *" | interval: ms like "3600000" | once: local ISO "2026-02-01T15:30:00" (no Z!)

context_mode.description: group=with chat history, isolated=fresh session (include context in prompt)

tools.name: refresh_groups
tools.description: Request a refresh of WhatsApp group metadata. Main group only. Call this when the list in available_groups seems stale or the user says they added you to a new group. No confirmation needed.

tools.name: available_groups
tools.description: Return the list of WhatsApp groups (from /workspace/ipc/available_groups.json). Shows which groups are registered and which are not. Use to find JIDs for register_group. No confirmation needed.

tools.name: read_group_messages
tools.description: Read recent message history from the current group chat. Use when the user asks for a summary of the conversation, what was said, or to catch up on messages. Returns up to 50 messages by default.

tools.name: register_group
tools.description: Register a new WhatsApp group so the bot can respond there. Main group only. Use available_groups to find the JID. When the user says they added you to a group, call this to activate it.

requiresTrigger.description: If false, bot replies to all messages. Default true for groups.

tools.name: list_scheduled_tasks
tools.description: List all scheduled tasks.

tools.name: pause_task
tools.description: Pause a scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: resume_task
tools.description: Resume a paused scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: cancel_task
tools.description: Cancel and delete a scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: reset_session
tools.description: Clear this chat's conversation history so the next message starts fresh. Use when the user asks to reset the session, clear memory, start over, or forget the conversation.

tools.name: store_memory
tools.description: Store a short note or summary for future turns (e.g. what you did, what the user asked, key facts). Use after important steps so context is preserved without sending huge history. Stored memory is injected into your context each turn.

content.description: Short summary or fact to remember (e.g. 'User asked for screenshot of aelf.org; I took page.png and sent it via send_image')

tools.name: consult_memory
tools.description: Return the current stored memory (recent summaries and facts from store_memory). Use when you need to recall prior context. Stored memory is also injected into your system prompt each turn.

tools.name: submit_plan
tools.description: Submit your execution plan before doing multi-step tasks. Call this first with an ordered list of steps; then execute each step with tool calls. Ensures efficient execution and no skipped steps (e.g. send_image after screenshot).

steps.description: Ordered list of steps, e.g. ["Open URL with agent-browser", "Screenshot page.png", "send_image(page.png)"]

tools.name: clear_plan
tools.description: Clear the current plan after you have executed it and stored a summary with store_memory. Call when the plan is done so the next turn starts fresh.

tools.name: kb_add
tools.description: Add a new knowledge base entry for this group. Perfect for company policies, contact info, project briefs, meeting notes, etc. Searchable later by kb_search.

title.description: Short title for the entry (e.g., 'WFH Policy', 'Contact: Legal', 'Project Alpha Brief')

content.description: Full text content of the entry

tags.description: Optional comma-separated tags for categorization (e.g., 'policy,hr' or 'project,planning')

tools.name: kb_search
tools.description: Search the knowledge base by keyword or semantic intent. Use this to find relevant entries before answering questions about company policies, contacts, or past discussions.

query.description: Search query (e.g., 'vacation policy', 'contact legal', 'project alpha timeline')

tools.name: kb_list
tools.description: List all knowledge base entries for this group. Call to see what you know about this group's context.

tools.name: add_task
tools.description: Add a task to the task list (secretary-style). Tasks are scoped to this group and can be todos, meetings, follow-ups, or reminders. Use for managing to-dos, scheduling, and tracking action items.

title.description: Task title (e.g., 'Organize meeting notes', 'Follow up on quote')

due_date.description: Optional due date in ISO format (e.g., '2026-03-10T09:00:00.000Z') or relative (e.g., 'next Friday 9am')

type.description: Task type: todo, meeting, follow_up, or reminder

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update an existing task. Use to mark as done, change due date, update title, or adjust priority.

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Delete a task from the task list.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: List tasks, optionally filtered by status or type. Use to see what's pending, what's due soon, or what's been completed.

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List all available workflows and automations in the registry. Call this when the user asks what you can do, what automations are available, or to browse capabilities.

tools.name: search_tools
tools.description: Search the workflow registry for tools/automations by keyword or intent. Returns matching entries with name, description, and how to run them. Call this first when the user asks you to do something that might be a pre-built workflow.

query.description: Keywords describing what you want to do, e.g. "morning briefing", "send slack message", "fetch leads"

tools.name: run_workflow
tools.description: Run a workflow by name from the registry. Optionally pass arguments as a JSON object.

args.description: Optional key-value arguments passed as environment variables to the script
